meta.title: Characterization of DNA Polymerase Activity in Trichoplusia ni Cells Infected with Autographa californica Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus

abstractHtml: Nuclei isolated from Trichoplusia ni cells (TN-368) infected with Autographa californica nuclear polyhedrosis virus (AcMNPV) were used to study the replication of viral DNA. Based on DNA:DNA hybridization data, virus-specific DNA polymerase activity was insensitive to aphidicolin and novobiocin and was inhibited by ddTTP and N-ethylmaleimide. The data indicate that the AcMNPV-specific DNA polymerase is a γ-like DNA polymerase which was first detected at 5 h post-inoculation.
